_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-6 Dead on Scene / Termination of Resuscitation (Revised 3/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Dead on Scene / Termination of Resuscitation
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-6: Dead on Scene / Termination of Resuscitation — MDHHS Approved 3/17/2025
purpose: Establish criteria for declaring a patient dead on scene (DOA) and for terminating resuscitation after adequate resuscitative efforts. Resuscitation is performed on scene for at least 30 minutes before termination is considered.

sections.title: Dead on Arrival (DOA) — Criteria
points: Any ONE of the following criteria qualifies as DOA (resuscitation should NOT be initiated):
1. Obvious signs of biological death: decomposition, rigor mortis, or dependent lividity
2. Decapitation
3. Traumatic cardiac arrest while entrapped (and patient cannot be extricated)
4. Incineration (burned beyond viable resuscitation) or frozen with core temperature incompatible with life
5. Cold water submersion > 90 minutes
6. Warm water submersion > 30 minutes
7. Gross dismemberment or mortal wounds incompatible with life
8. Unwitnessed traumatic cardiac arrest without organized electrical activity (asystole or pulseless rhythm < 40/min) — EXCEPTION: electrocution or hypothermia
9. Valid DNR bracelet or form (refer to DNR-Procedure Protocol)
10. MI-POST form with Section A selecting DO NOT attempt resuscitation
11. MCI setting: patient pulseless and apneic (triage per MCI protocol)

sections.title: Exceptions — Resuscitate Despite DOA Criteria
points: EMS professional judgment determines that resuscitation is appropriate despite DOA criteria
Witnessed cardiac arrest of a pregnant patient — initiate resuscitation AND transport immediately; contact Medical Control

sections.title: Resuscitation on Scene
points: Resuscitation should be performed ON SCENE for at least 30 minutes before termination of resuscitation is considered
Continue resuscitation per Cardiac Arrest protocol throughout resuscitative efforts
Do NOT transport unless: ROSC achieved, Medical Control orders transport, or BLS/ALS termination criteria not met

sections.title: ALS / LALS Termination of Resuscitation (TOR) Criteria
points: After 30 minutes of ALS on-scene resuscitation time:
— Asystole present in all 3 leads OR PEA rate < 40/min: consider termination of resuscitation
— PEA rate ≥ 40/min or persistent/recurrent ventricular fibrillation: contact Medical Control for termination order or further orders
Contact Medical Control to notify of termination
Document time of death, hospital name contacted, physician name, law enforcement notification status, and Gift of Life status inquiry

sections.title: BLS Termination of Resuscitation (TOR) Criteria
points: ALL THREE criteria must be met for BLS TOR:
1. Unwitnessed cardiac arrest (by bystanders or EMS)
2. No ROSC after 20 minutes of high-quality CPR
3. No AED shock was delivered
If criteria met: contact Medical Control for termination order
Document time of death, hospital name contacted, physician name, law enforcement notification, and Gift of Life status

sections.title: Documentation Requirements
points: Document time of death
Document name of hospital and physician contacted for Medical Control authorization
Document law enforcement notification
Document Gift of Life (organ donation) status inquiry or notification
Document circumstances of arrest: witnessed vs. unwitnessed, downtime estimate, bystander CPR, AED use
Document all resuscitative interventions and times

deterioration_flags: Asystole in all 3 leads after 30 min ALS resuscitation — consider ALS TOR; contact Medical Control
PEA < 40/min after 30 min ALS — consider ALS TOR; contact Medical Control
PEA ≥ 40/min or recurrent V-fib — contact Medical Control for further orders before TOR
BLS with 20 min HQCPR, no ROSC, no AED shock, unwitnessed — contact Medical Control for BLS TOR
Pregnant patient — resuscitate and transport immediately even if DOA criteria present (except obvious biological death)
scope_notes: All EMS provider levels may declare DOA when criteria are met
Termination of resuscitation requires Medical Control authorization
ALS TOR: after 30 min ALS resuscitation time — asystole all 3 leads or PEA < 40/min
BLS TOR: unwitnessed + no ROSC after 20 min HQCPR + no AED shock
Pregnant patient witnessed arrest: always resuscitate and transport — exception to DOA
Law enforcement must be notified of all field deaths
